title: Daily Briefing — May 12
headline: Tech leads, but bond breakdowns and lagging sectors flash early warning — patience, react, don't predict.
body: Right, so here's what the flows are telling us: SPY and QQQ pushing toward new highs, with the 20 MA still comfortably below price (SPY: 715.72, QQQ: 665.59). Fear & Greed is at 65.9 — firmly Greed, but not extreme. VIX expanding above its 20 MA (18.85 vs 18.1), suggesting rising volatility just as indices test resistance.

XLK remains firmly in the Leading quadrant per sector RRG (RS=117.4, Mom=100.9), but every other major sector is lagging or stuck with HIGH risk. That’s classic narrow breadth — tech is dragging the rest of the market up while Energy, Materials, Industrials, Staples, and Healthcare all signal severe lag. XLF (Financials) is the canary; risk is elevated but it’s improving, so keep an eye on the weekly 20 MA here.

Now for the contrarian flag: Bond proxies HYG, TLT, and LQD all approaching or triggering bear bias with daily closes below their 20 MA. Rule R001 says reduce equity exposure if bonds break down — we've got TLT triggered (85.56 < 86.01). When the canary (XLF) is wobbly and bond flows are negative, don't ignore it. Also, if it's in the press, it's in the price — all eyes on tech just as lag is broadening. Abundance mindset: don't chase.
callout_body:
SPY: Weekly 20 MA 684.83 — mean reversion zone
XLK: Watch 160.39 (daily 20 MA)
XLF: 51.87 (daily 20 MA), 52.29 (SMA200) — critical support
HYG: Bear bias active, daily close below 80.01
TLT: Triggered bear, daily close below 86.01
VIX: Above 20 MA, volatility is rising
NVDA: Strong bull bias, watch 204.69 (SMA20) for pullbacks
action_items: Reduce equity exposure on any fresh daily or weekly bond breakdowns; watch for HYG/LQD/TLT confirming moves.
Do NOT chase tech — focus on the mean reversion zone (SPY 684.83, XLK 160.39) for best risk/reward. If missed, wait for next setup.
closing_quote: If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Patience, react, don’t predict.
